ExtractAgent Extraction complète initiale : j'ai identifié cinq arguments principaux (A1–A5). Chaque argument est formulé comme claim générale présente dans le discours. J'ai extrait aussi plusieurs assertions factuelles (e.g., $17 trillion investments; zero illegal entries for 4 months; 48 record highs in stock market; 300,000 drug deaths) qui peuvent servir comme prémisses/supports ou cibles de vérification. ['source:rev_transcript_2026-05-15']
[] []
ca_1 Arg1: Protecting American jobs requires restricting immigration and renegotiating trade deals to stop unfair competition. <scrubbed> Rhetorical strategy: factual refutation + analytic reframing. Show empirical nuance, concede localized harms then propose targeted policy alternatives (concession + constructive alternative). 0.78 Arg1
ca_2 Arg4: Tax cuts for corporations and the wealthy stimulate investment and economic growth, benefiting everyone. <scrubbed> Rhetorical strategy: empirical undermining + policy redirection. Show that intended mechanism (tax cuts→investment→jobs) is empirically weak and propose tied incentives instead. 0.73 Arg4
ca_3 Arg7: Immigration increases crime and threatens national security. <scrubbed> Rhetorical strategy: factual correction + moral framing. Use statistical evidence to debunk the causal claim and highlight policy alternatives that balance security and civil liberties. 0.82 Arg7
ca_4 Arg10: Globalism harms American sovereignty and culture; we need nationalism. <scrubbed> Rhetorical strategy: concession + comparative framing. Accept legitimate sovereignty concerns, then show superior alternatives to isolation—policy instruments that retain benefits while reducing costs. 0.69 Arg10
